system.name: Monster of the Week
system.description: A standalone action-horror RPG. Hunters track down and destroy the monsters that most people don't believe in. Powered by the Apocalypse engine.

pitch.hook: You build the monster. They have to survive it.
pitch.genreNote: If you love Supernatural, Buffy, or The X-Files — no rules knowledge needed. Just show up.

audienceActions.label: Name the victims
audienceActions.detail: You decide who lives in this town, what they're hiding, and who gets got first.

audienceActions.label: Build the monster
audienceActions.detail: Vote on what it looks like, what it hunts, and the one thing that can kill it.

audienceActions.label: Drive every bad call
audienceActions.detail: When the hunters face an impossible choice, the room decides. When it gets really bad, everyone rolls together.

list.name: Charm
list.description: How pleasant and persuasive you are.

list.name: Cool
list.description: How calm and collected you are.

list.name: Tough
list.description: How strong and mean you are in a fight.

list.name: Weird
list.description: How attuned to the supernatural you are.

mechanic.default: Roll 2d6 and add the relevant rating.

results.10+: Full success. You do what you set out to do.
results.7-9: Partial success. You succeed with a cost, hard choice, or worse outcome.
results.6-: Miss. The Keeper makes a move against you. Also mark experience.
results.12+: Only matters for advanced moves — grants an extraordinary result.

bonuses.forward: +1 (or stated amount) to your very next roll only.
bonuses.ongoing: +1 (or stated amount) to every relevant roll until the situation changes.
bonuses.hold: A pool of points spent one-for-one for listed effects.

spendOptions: After you roll, retroactively change the result to a 12.
Decrease a wound you just suffered to 0-harm, no matter how bad it was.
luck.whenEmpty: The hunter is 'doomed'. The Keeper may make hard moves against them at any time.

basicMoves.name: Act Under Pressure
basicMoves.trigger: You try to do something under stress or danger not covered by another move.

results.10+: You do what you set out to.
results.7-9: The Keeper gives you a worse outcome, hard choice, or price to pay.

basicMoves.name: Help Out
basicMoves.trigger: You help another hunter with a move they are making.

results.10+: Your help grants +1 to their roll.
results.7-9: Your help grants +1 to their roll, but you expose yourself to trouble.
results.6-: You expose yourself to trouble without helping.

basicMoves.name: Investigate a Mystery
basicMoves.trigger: You investigate to learn about what's going on.

results.6-: You reveal information to the monster or whoever you're talking to.

holdQuestions: What sort of creature is it?
What was it going to do?

basicMoves.name: Kick Some Ass
basicMoves.trigger: You get into a fight with something that's fighting back.

results.7+: You and your enemy inflict harm on each other.
results.10+: As 7-9, plus choose one: gain advantage (+1 forward); inflict terrible harm (+1 harm); suffer less harm (-1 harm); force them where you want.

basicMoves.name: Manipulate Someone
basicMoves.trigger: You want someone to do something for you, and you give them a reason.

results.10+: They'll do it for the reason given.
results.7-9: They'll do it, but only if you do something right now to show you mean it.
results.6-: Your approach is wrong — you offend or anger them.

basicMoves.name: Protect Someone
basicMoves.trigger: Someone is about to suffer harm and you try to prevent it.

results.7+: You protect them, but suffer some or all of the harm they were going to get.
results.10+: Choose one extra: suffer little harm (-1); focus danger on you; inflict harm on the enemy; hold the enemy back.

basicMoves.name: Read a Bad Situation
basicMoves.trigger: You look around and assess a hostile or dangerous situation.

results.6-: You misread it, or reveal tactical details to your enemies.

holdQuestions: Are there any dangers we haven't noticed?
What's the best way to protect the victims?

basicMoves.name: Use Magic
basicMoves.trigger: You cast a spell or use an enchanted item.

results.7-9: It works imperfectly — choose your effect and the Keeper adds a glitch.
results.6-: You lose control of the magic.

playbooks.name: The Chosen
playbooks.concept: A once-ordinary person with a prophesied destiny and the power to fulfill it.

moves.name: Destiny's Plaything
moves.description: At the start of each mystery, roll +Weird: 10+ the Keeper reveals a useful detail; 7-9 a vague hint; miss something bad happens to you.

moves.name: I'm Here For A Reason
moves.description: You're destined to do something; you can't die until it comes to pass (spend Luck if you would die). Once your task is done, all bets are off.

moves.name: The Big Entrance
moves.description: Make a showy entrance — roll +Cool to make enemies stop and listen.

moves.name: Devastating
moves.description: When you inflict harm, you may inflict +1 harm.

moves.name: Dutiful
moves.description: Act in accordance with a heroic fate tag to mark experience and gain +1 forward.

moves.name: Invincible
moves.description: Always count as having 2-armour (doesn't stack with worn armour).

moves.name: Resilience
moves.description: Heal an extra point whenever healed; wounds count as 1-harm less for Keeper harm moves.

gear: A special destined weapon (custom-built during creation)
playbooks.luckSpecial: When you spend Luck, the Keeper brings your fate into play.
playbooks.advancedImprovement: Delete one doom tag from your fate.

fate.description: Your character has a Fate with heroic and doom tags that shape your destiny.
fate.pick: Choose how you found out, 2 heroic tags, and 2 doom tags.

specialWeapon.description: Build your destined weapon: choose a form, three business-end options, and a material.

playbooks.name: The Crooked
playbooks.concept: A criminal turned monster hunter, haunted by their past and enemies.

moves.name: Artifact
moves.description: You found and kept a magical artifact: protective amulet, lucky charm, grimoire, skeleton key, or imp stone.

moves.name: Crew
moves.description: You have a regular crew of three or four who help with anything (counts as a team).

moves.name: Deal with the Devil
moves.description: You sold your soul; pick one or two things you got. Payment is due eventually.

moves.name: Friends on the Force
moves.description: Act under pressure to contact cops who'll look the other way (for a cost).

moves.name: Made
moves.description: You're 'made' in a gang; you can call on members but owe loyalty.

moves.name: Driver
moves.description: +1 ongoing while driving; can hotwire anything; own two handy vehicles.

moves.name: Home Ground
moves.description: In your old neighbourhood you can always find people to hide or help you.

moves.name: Notorious
moves.description: Your criminal reputation counts as leverage when you reveal who you are.

gear: Three weapons (choose from: .22, .38, 9mm, shotgun, rifle, knife, bat, SMG, assault rifle)
playbooks.luckSpecial: When you spend Luck, someone from your past reappears soon.

background.description: What kind of criminal were you before hunting monsters?

heat.description: Name at least two enemies from your criminal past — people who want to make your life difficult.

underworld.description: How did you discover that monsters are real? Pick an event that brought you into the real underworld.

playbooks.name: The Divine
playbooks.concept: An agent of a higher power with a mission. Very tough, with holy powers.

moves.name: Boss from Beyond
moves.description: At the start of a mystery, roll +Weird to receive (and question) orders from your Superiors.

moves.name: Angel Wings
moves.description: Teleport to anywhere you've visited or a person you know; roll +Weird to carry others.

moves.name: What I Need, When I Need It
moves.description: Store small objects in a magical space and retrieve them at will.

moves.name: Soothe
moves.description: Calm someone with a few quiet words, blocking panic, anger, or negative emotions.

moves.name: Lay On Hands
moves.description: Roll +Cool to heal injury or disease by touch.

moves.name: Cast Out Evil
moves.description: Roll +Tough to banish an unnatural creature from your presence.

moves.name: Smite
moves.description: Your body and divine weapon count as a weakness vs monsters; unarmed attacks are 2-harm intimate hand messy.

gear: One divine weapon (flaming sword / thunder hammer / razor whip / five demon bag / silver trident — all holy)

mission.description: Why were you sent here? Choose your divine mission.
options: Fight the schemes of an Adversary
Ensure the End of Days comes to pass
Work for Good in exile, hidden from your kind
Prepare and protect a specific other hunter

playbooks.name: The Expert
playbooks.concept: The hunter who knows all about monsters and magic, with a well-supplied base.

moves.name: I've Read About This Sort Of Thing
moves.description: Roll +Sharp instead of +Cool when you act under pressure.

moves.name: Often Right
moves.description: Give honest advice; if a hunter follows it they get +1 ongoing and you mark experience.

moves.name: Preparedness
moves.description: Roll +Sharp to have an unusual or rare item on hand.

moves.name: It Wasn't As Bad As It Looked
moves.description: Once per mystery, roll +Cool to keep going despite injuries.

moves.name: Precise Strike
moves.description: Roll +Tough to aim for a weak spot and inflict bonus harm.

moves.name: The Woman (or Man) With The Plan
moves.description: At the start of a mystery, roll +Sharp to hold and spend to be prepared and in the right place.

moves.name: Dark Past
moves.description: Roll +Weird to recall relevant black-magic knowledge (with a risk of complicity).

gear: Three monster-slaying weapons (stakes / silver sword / cold iron sword / blessed knife / magical dagger / juju bag / flamethrower / magnum / shotgun)

haven.description: You have a haven — a base of operations. Pick 3 features from the list.

playbooks.name: The Flake
playbooks.concept: A conspiracy theorist, great at finding things out and connecting separate mysteries.
examples: Agent Mulder / The Lone Gunmen (The X-Files)

moves.name: Connect the Dots
moves.description: At the start of a mystery, roll +Sharp to hold and spend asking how events connect to the bigger picture.

moves.name: Crazy Eyes
moves.description: +1 Weird (max +3).

moves.name: See, It All Fits Together
moves.description: Use Sharp instead of Charm when you manipulate someone.

moves.name: Suspicious Mind
moves.description: If someone lies to you, you know it.

moves.name: Often Overlooked
moves.description: Roll +Weird to act crazy and be regarded as unthreatening or unimportant.

moves.name: Contrary
moves.description: Seek honest advice then do something else to mark experience (+1 ongoing if you do the opposite).

moves.name: Net Friends
moves.description: Roll +Charm to get internet contacts to help with a mystery.

moves.name: Sneaky
moves.description: Attack from ambush or behind for +2 harm.

playbooks.name: The Initiate
playbooks.concept: A trained member of an ancient monster-slaying Sect. Good with magic; the Sect provides help and problems.

moves.name: Sect Standing
moves.description: When in good standing, start of mystery roll +Charm for Sect help; a miss means they ask something bad.

moves.name: Ancient Fighting Arts
moves.description: +1 harm and +1 to protect someone with an old-fashioned hand weapon.

moves.name: Mystic
moves.description: +1 forward every time you successfully use magic.

moves.name: Fortunes
moves.description: Once per mystery, roll +Weird to hold and spend on prophecy and divination effects.

moves.name: Sacred Oath
moves.description: Bind yourself to a goal and forsake something; mark experience and +1 on goal-related rolls.

moves.name: Mentor
moves.description: Roll +Sharp to get answers from your named Sect mentor.

moves.name: Apprentice
moves.description: You have a named apprentice who counts as a subordinate ally.

moves.name: Helping Hand
moves.description: Successful help out gives +2 instead of +1.

moves.name: That Old Black Magic
moves.description: Use magic to ask an investigate-a-mystery question as your effect.

playbooks.advancedImprovement: Become the leader, or effective leader, of the whole Sect.

sect.description: Define your Sect — the ancient order you belong to. Pick 2 good traditions and 1 bad tradition.

playbooks.name: The Monstrous
playbooks.concept: A monster fighting for the good guys. Very weird, with powers based on its breed.

moves.name: Immortal
moves.description: You don't age or sicken, and suffer 1-harm less whenever harmed.

moves.name: Unnatural Appeal
moves.description: Roll +Weird instead of +Charm when you manipulate someone.

moves.name: Unholy Strength
moves.description: Roll +Weird instead of +Tough when you kick some ass.

moves.name: Incorporeal
moves.description: Move freely through solid objects (not people).

moves.name: Preternatural Speed
moves.description: +1 ongoing when you chase, flee, or run.

moves.name: Claws of the Beast
moves.description: All natural attacks get +1 harm.

moves.name: Mental Dominion
moves.description: Roll +Charm to hold and command normal humans.

moves.name: Unquenchable Vitality
moves.description: Roll +Cool to heal yourself.

moves.name: Dark Negotiator
moves.description: Use manipulate someone on monsters that reason and talk.

moves.name: Flight
moves.description: You can fly.

moves.name: Shapeshifter
moves.description: Change form; +1 to investigate a mystery using an alternate form's senses.

moves.name: Something Borrowed
moves.description: Take a move from a hunter playbook not currently in play.

playbooks.advancedImprovement: Free yourself from the curse (lose 1 Weird); or turn evil again (retire as a Keeper threat).

breed.description: What kind of monster are you? Pick your breed.

curses.description: Pick one or two curses that define your monster nature.
options: Feed (you must regularly feed on something — blood, fear, souls, etc.)
Vulnerability (something can hurt you badly — sunlight, silver, salt, etc.)
Pure Drive (one overwhelming compulsion — hunger, violence, lust, etc.)
Dark Master (someone or something has a hold over you)

naturalAttacks.description: Choose a base and optionally add extras.
extras: An extra range on a base

playbooks.name: The Mundane
playbooks.concept: A normal person, great with regular people and surprisingly useful in a crisis.

moves.name: Always The Victim
moves.description: When protected, the protector marks experience. When captured by a monster, you mark experience.

moves.name: Oops!
moves.description: Stumble across something important and useful (not necessarily related to your problem).

moves.name: Let's Get Out Of Here!
moves.description: Protect someone by leading them out, rolling +Charm instead of +Tough.

moves.name: Panic Button
moves.description: Roll +Sharp to escape via a named route.

moves.name: The Power of Heart
moves.description: When you help someone fighting a monster, you automatically help as a 10+.

moves.name: Trust Me
moves.description: Roll +Charm to tell a normal person the truth to protect them.

moves.name: What Could Go Wrong?
moves.description: Charge into danger to hold 2 for combat effects.

moves.name: Don't Worry, I'll Check It Out
moves.description: Go off alone somewhere scary to mark experience.

playbooks.name: The Professional
playbooks.concept: You work for an agency that hunts monsters. Good in a fight and a strong team player.

moves.name: Deal with the Agency
moves.description: Roll +Sharp when requesting help/gear or making excuses to the Agency.

moves.name: Bottle It Up
moves.description: Take up to +3 on act under pressure; the Keeper holds 1 per +1 for later penalties.

moves.name: Unfazeable
moves.description: +1 Cool (max +3).

moves.name: Battlefield Awareness
moves.description: +1 armour (max 2) on top of gear.

moves.name: Leave No One Behind
moves.description: Roll +Sharp to help someone escape combat cleanly.

moves.name: Tactical Genius
moves.description: Roll +Cool instead of +Sharp when you read a bad situation.

moves.name: Medic
moves.description: Roll +Cool for first aid; replaces regular first aid.

moves.name: Mobility
moves.description: A monster-hunting vehicle with two good and one bad trait.

gear: Flak vest (1-armour hidden) or combat armour (2-armour heavy)
playbooks.advancedImprovement: Get some or all of the other hunters hired by your agency.

agency.description: Define your Agency. Pick 2 resource tags and 2 red tape tags.

playbooks.name: The Spell-Slinger
playbooks.concept: A trained wizard wielding powerful combat magic. Arcane training helps with eldritch secrets.

moves.name: Tools and Techniques
moves.description: Combat magic relies on Consumables, Foci, Gestures, and Incantations. Cross one off; the rest have drawbacks if unavailable.

moves.name: Advanced Arcane Training
moves.description: If you have two of three remaining Tools and Techniques ready, ignore the third.

moves.name: Arcane Reputation
moves.description: Three supernatural groups respect you; +1 forward to manipulate affected humans.

moves.name: Could've Been Worse
moves.description: On a use-magic miss, choose Fizzle or This Is Gonna Suck instead of losing control.

moves.name: Enchanted Clothing
moves.description: An enchanted garment gives -1 harm from sources that reach you through it.

moves.name: Forensic Divination
moves.description: On a successful investigate, ask 'What magic was done here?' for free.

moves.name: Go Big or Go Home
moves.description: +1 ongoing to use-magic rolls required for Big Magic.

moves.name: Not My Fault
moves.description: +1 to act under pressure dealing with your own spellcasting consequences.

moves.name: Practitioner
moves.description: Choose two use-magic effects; +1 to use magic when choosing them.

moves.name: Shield Spell
moves.description: When you protect someone, gain 2-armour against transferred harm.

moves.name: Third Eye
moves.description: When you read a bad situation, +1 hold on 7+ and see invisible things (miss exposes you).

gear: One backup weapon (old revolver / ritual knife / heirloom sword)
playbooks.advancedImprovement: Cross off another option from your Tools and Techniques.

toolsAndTechniques.description: Your combat magic requires four elements. Cross one off — you've eliminated that requirement. The remaining three are needed; if one is unavailable, magic has drawbacks.

combatMagic.description: Pick 3 options total, at least 1 must be a base. Pick effects separately.
bases: Ball (1-harm magical area close obvious loud)
Wall (1-harm magical barrier close 1-armour obvious loud)

playbooks.name: The Spooky
playbooks.concept: Has strange, sinister psychic or magical powers not fully under their control.

moves.name: Telepathy
moves.description: Read thoughts and project words; investigate, read situations, and manipulate without speaking.

moves.name: Hex
moves.description: When you use magic, add a disease, immediate harm, or breakage to the target.

moves.name: The Sight
moves.description: See and communicate with the invisible; more clues when you investigate.

moves.name: Premonitions
moves.description: At the start of a mystery, roll +Weird for a vision of something bad to come.

moves.name: Hunches
moves.description: Roll +Sharp to know where to be when something bad happens elsewhere.

moves.name: Tune In
moves.description: Roll +Weird to attune to a monster or minion and hold for questions.

moves.name: The Big Whammy
moves.description: Kick some ass with +Weird; 2-harm close obvious ignore-armour (miss = backlash).

moves.name: Jinx
moves.description: Roll +Weird to encourage coincidences and hold for effects.

gear: Magical items or amulets used to invoke powers
playbooks.luckSpecial: As you spend Luck, your dark side's demands grow nastier.
playbooks.advancedImprovement: Delete one dark side tag permanently.

darkSide.description: Your powers have a dark side — 3 tags that define the shadow you carry. These will come into play when Luck is spent or when the Keeper wants to complicate your life.

playbooks.name: The Wronged
playbooks.concept: Revenge-driven and very tough. Focused on killing a specific breed of monster and protecting others.

moves.name: I Know My Prey
moves.description: +1 ongoing when knowingly investigating, pursuing, or fighting the monster breed that caused your loss.

moves.name: Berserk
moves.description: Keep going until the fight ends; the Keeper can't use harm moves and you can't die mid-fight (harm lands afterward).

moves.name: NEVER AGAIN
moves.description: Protect someone without rolling as a 10+, but can't choose 'suffer little harm'.

moves.name: What Does Not Kill Me...
moves.description: +1 ongoing until the fight ends once you've suffered harm in it.

moves.name: Fervor
moves.description: Roll +Tough instead of +Charm to manipulate someone.

moves.name: Safety First
moves.description: +1 armour (max 2) from jury-rigged gear.

moves.name: DIY Surgery
moves.description: Roll +Cool for quick and dirty first aid on yourself or others.

moves.name: Tools Matter
moves.description: +1 to kick some ass with your signature weapon.

playbooks.advancedImprovement: Track down the specific monster(s) responsible; or change the target of I Know My Prey.

loss.description: Define what drove you to hunt. Who did you lose, what killed them, and why couldn't you stop it?
loss.prey: Decide with your Keeper which specific monster breed took what you love.

standard: Get +1 to a rating (up to the playbook max).
Take another move from your playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Gain an ally or a team.
Gain a haven or add a haven option.
advanced: Change your hunter to a new playbook type.
Create a second hunter to play as well.
Mark two basic moves as advanced (unlocks 12+ results).
improvements.leveling: Mark experience on a miss (6-) or when a move says to. Five marks = level up.
endOfSessionQuestions: Did we conclude the current mystery?
Did we save someone from certain death (or worse)?
Did we learn something new and important about the world?
Did we learn something new and important about one of the hunters?
